skill.name: pptx
skill.description: Create, edit, inspect, validate, and convert PowerPoint presentations using PPTX, HTML-to-PPTX, and OOXML workflows.
skill.summary: Build and analyze PowerPoint decks with HTML, PPTXGenJS, OOXML, thumbnails, and validation scripts.
skill.author: Cam10001110101

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer reported many command, network, filesystem, and keyword findings. Most keyword, URL, and documentation hits are false positives, but the bundled scripts perform unsafe archive extraction on Office files and the skill instructs users to run system commands, so publication should wait for remediation.

high_findings.title: Unsafe archive extraction of Office files
high_findings.description: The unpacking and validation scripts call ZipFile.extractall on user-supplied Office documents without checking member paths. A crafted PPTX, DOCX, or XLSX archive could write files outside the intended output directory through Zip Slip path traversal.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The finding is based on direct extractall calls on Office archives supplied through script inputs. No path normalization or member validation is visible at the cited extraction sites.

medium_findings.title: External command workflows need user trust boundaries
medium_findings.description: The skill directs agents to run Node, npm, LibreOffice, Poppler, and generated presentation scripts. The Python subprocess calls use argument arrays rather than shell strings, but generated JavaScript and local document conversion still execute code and process untrusted presentation content.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The external command usage is real and central to the skill. The risk is reduced because several subprocess calls are fixed argument lists, but the workflow still executes generated files and system tools.

medium_findings.title: Privileged dependency installation instructions
medium_findings.description: The dependency section recommends sudo apt-get installs for LibreOffice and Poppler. This is not malicious by itself, but marketplace users should not be asked to grant elevated privileges without clear review and environment controls.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines explicitly include sudo installation commands. They are common package installation commands, so the concern is operational risk rather than confirmed abuse.

low_findings.title: Static keyword findings are mostly false positives
low_findings.description: Many high-severity weak cryptography, C2 keyword, reconnaissance, obfuscation, and hardcoded URL hits occur in Markdown examples, OOXML schema names, namespaces, or validation terminology. I did not find evidence that these are malicious behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The inspected examples are schema namespaces, OOXML terms, font discovery paths, and documentation references. Static labels are noisy here, though the whole package still needs review because real script risks exist.

low_findings.title: Temporary directory environment access appears benign
low_findings.description: The documented html2pptx option reads TMPDIR only to choose a temporary working directory. No evidence found that environment variables are exfiltrated or used as secrets.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited context describes a tmpDir option and defaults to process.env.TMPDIR or /tmp. This is normal temporary directory configuration, not credential access.

dangerous_patterns.title: Zip archive extraction without member validation
dangerous_patterns.description: Multiple scripts extract Office archives directly with extractall. Replace this with safe extraction that rejects absolute paths and paths escaping the destination directory.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Direct ZipFile.extractall calls are visible at the cited lines. The surrounding code does not show member path filtering before extraction.

dangerous_patterns.title: Generated script execution path
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill instructs users to generate and run JavaScript that creates PPTX files. This pattern is expected for the skill, but it should be limited to trusted workspaces and reviewed before execution.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The instruction to run node on a generated script is explicit. The risk depends on the generated script contents and workspace trust level.

content.value_statement: PowerPoint work often requires layout control, file inspection, and reliable conversion steps. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through PPTX creation, editing, validation, and thumbnail review.
actual_capabilities: Create new PPTX presentations from HTML slide files and PPTXGenJS scripts.
Inspect raw OOXML files inside PPTX archives for slides, notes, layouts, comments, themes, and media.
Modify existing presentations by unpacking, editing XML, validating structure, and repacking files.
Generate thumbnail grids from presentations to check layout, contrast, cutoff, and overlap issues.
Use html2pptx guidance for CSS variables, slide dimensions, placeholders, charts, tables, and speaker notes.
Validate OOXML relationships, content types, notes slides, redlining, and package structure with bundled scripts.
limitations: The bundled archive extraction scripts need Zip Slip protection before handling untrusted Office files.
Several workflows require local tools such as Node, npm packages, LibreOffice, Poppler, Python, and defusedxml.
Complex PowerPoint animations and proprietary effects may require direct OOXML work and manual review.
Visual quality depends on thumbnail inspection and iterative fixes, not automatic design judgment alone.

use_cases.title: Build executive presentations
use_cases.description: Create a structured PowerPoint deck with consistent typography, colors, charts, tables, and speaker notes.

use_cases.title: Repair or update existing decks
use_cases.description: Unpack a presentation, edit slide text or notes, validate OOXML relationships, and repack the file.

use_cases.title: Audit presentation quality
use_cases.description: Generate slide thumbnails and inspect text cutoff, overlap, visual contrast, hidden slides, and layout consistency.

prompt_templates.title: Create a basic deck
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a five-slide PPTX about quarterly results. Use a 16:9 layout, clear titles, concise bullets, and speaker notes for each slide.

prompt_templates.title: Edit an existing presentation
prompt_templates.prompt: Open this PPTX, summarize its structure, update slide titles for clarity, preserve the theme, and produce a revised file.

prompt_templates.title: Validate and thumbnail a deck
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate thumbnails for this PPTX, inspect every slide for cutoff or overlap, list the issues, and fix the deck where possible.

prompt_templates.title: Advanced OOXML repair
prompt_templates.prompt: Unpack this PPTX, inspect slide relationships, notes slides, content types, and theme references, then repair structural issues and validate the result.

output_examples.input: Create a product roadmap presentation from an outline.
output_examples.output: A PowerPoint deck with title, roadmap, milestone, risk, and next-step slides, plus speaker notes and consistent styling.

output_examples.input: Review this sales deck for layout problems.
output: Thumbnail grid created for visual review.
Findings list covering cutoff text, overlap, contrast, hidden slides, and inconsistent spacing.
A revised PPTX when issues can be corrected safely.

output_examples.input: Extract the text and notes from this presentation.
output_examples.output: A readable summary of slide titles, body text, speaker notes, comments, and key theme details.

best_practices: Use trusted input files until archive extraction is hardened against path traversal.
Review generated JavaScript before running Node commands in a workspace.
Always validate the final deck with thumbnails before delivering it.
anti_patterns: Do not run sudo package installation commands in shared or production environments without approval.
Do not unpack Office files from unknown sources with the current extractall-based scripts.
Do not skip visual thumbnail review when creating layout-heavy decks.

faq.question: Can this skill create PowerPoint files from scratch?
faq.answer: Yes. It uses an HTML-to-PPTX workflow with PPTXGenJS for slides, charts, tables, and notes.

faq.question: Can it edit existing PPTX files?
faq.answer: Yes. It can inspect and modify raw OOXML, then validate and repack the presentation.

faq.question: Does it require external software?
faq.answer: Many workflows require Node, npm packages, Python scripts, LibreOffice, Poppler, and related local dependencies.

faq.question: Is it safe for untrusted presentations?
faq.answer: Not yet. The current archive extraction scripts need path validation before processing untrusted Office files.

faq.question: Can it check visual quality?
faq.answer: Yes. It can create slide thumbnails and guide review for overlap, cutoff, contrast, and layout problems.

faq.question: Does it work with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The marketplace metadata lists Claude, Codex, and Claude Code as supported tools.
